Name: Geoscientific model development
LongName: The publication of geoscientific model developments (GMD editorial framework, code/data policy, and review criteria)
Description: A geoscientific-model reporting framework derived from GMD editorial policy, manuscript-type framework, code/data policy, publication guide, and review criteria. It emphasizes model traceability, reproducibility, code/data accessibility, and rigorous evaluation.
Aim: To ensure manuscripts describing geoscientific model developments are reproducible, traceable, technically rigorous, and review-ready, with explicit code/data availability and transparent model-version reporting.
Keywords: model name and version in title

Scope.Coverage: Manuscripts in geoscientific numerical modeling that describe models, model developments, assessment methods, experiment protocols, and model-evaluation studies under GMD-style editorial requirements.
Scope.Focus: Model/version traceability, code/data availability, reproducible model and method description, benchmark/observation evaluation, clear manuscript classification, and editorial-quality checks at submission and review stages.
Scope.ContrastWith: Not intended for general geoscience result papers that do not primarily describe modelling methodology, model development, experiment protocols, or reproducibility-relevant computational details.

primary.description: GMD Executive Editors. Editorial: The publication of geoscientific model developments v1.1. Geosci. Model Dev. 2015;8:3487-3495.

secondary.description: Geoscientific Model Development review criteria (peer-review checklist and principal criteria).

items.item_question: Does the manuscript provide a substantial contribution to modelling science within scope?
items.item_question_background: Papers should contribute tangible advances in concepts, methods, tools, or modelling protocols.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract explicit statements of modelling novelty and substantive contribution relative to existing methods.

source_page_refs.quote: The review itself should focus on the clarity and rigour of the model description or development...

items.item_question: Are methods and assumptions valid, and are results discussed in a balanced way with appropriate references?
items.item_question_background: Technical validity and balanced interpretation are required for reliable model publications.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract method validity statements, assumption disclosures, and discussion of related work and references.

source_page_refs.quote: Papers should... present a significant advance, and be clearly focussed.

items.item_question: Is the modelling description complete and precise enough for traceable reproduction?
items.item_question_background: Traceability and reproducibility are core goals of the framework.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract passages about reproducibility, traceability, and whether independent reconstruction is feasible in principle.
items.item_expected_location: Methods / Model description / Code and data availability

source_page_refs.quote: ...models to be published such that they are scientifically reproducible...

items.item_question: Is the manuscript clearly structured, concise, and technically readable?
items.item_question_background: Clear structure and language are part of formal review quality.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract evidence of manuscript structure quality, figure/table clarity, and language precision considerations.

source_page_refs.quote: ...methods, results, and conclusions presented in a clear, concise, and well-structured way...

items.review_reason: This item is partly sourced from companion webpage criteria; verify exact wording alignment.

items.item_name: Model name and version in title
items.item_question: Does the title include model name and version (or unique identifier) when applicable?
items.item_question_background: Versioned titles are essential for traceability across developments.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract model/experiment identifiers from title and confirm explicit versioning.

source_page_refs.quote: The main paper must give the model name and version number... in the title.

items.item_question: Does the submission include main paper, user manual, and source code (with test outputs ideally)?
items.item_question_background: Reproducibility depends on documentation and executable artifacts.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether manuscript, user manual, source code, and representative test outputs are provided or linked.

source_page_refs.quote: The publication should consist of three parts: the main paper, a user manual, and the source code...

items.item_question: Is the scientific basis and numerical solution approach described in enough detail for reimplementation in principle?
items.item_question_background: Insufficient detail blocks independent reconstruction and validation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract scientific rationale, equations/algorithms, and technical details that materially affect numerical output.

source_page_refs.quote: ...description should be sufficiently detailed to in principle allow for the re-implementation of the model...

items.item_question: Are model URL, hardware/software requirements, and license details provided?
items.item_question_background: Operational context is needed to run and assess computational reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract model homepage/repository URL, platform/dependency requirements, and licensing information.

source_page_refs.quote: The model webpage URL, the hardware and software requirements and the license information should be given...

items.item_question: Is model applicability and limitation context discussed appropriately?
items.item_question_background: Users must understand valid application boundaries.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements on scope, assumptions, intended use, and known limitations.

source_page_refs.quote: The model description should be contextualised appropriately... scope of applicability and limitations...

items.item_question: Are outputs evaluated against benchmarks/data/models, with verification and evaluation distinguished?
items.item_question_background: Both numerical correctness and real-system representativeness should be addressed.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract benchmark and observational evaluation evidence and explicit distinction between verification and evaluation.

source_page_refs.quote: ...distinguish between verification... and evaluation...

items.item_question: Is a mandatory code and/or data availability section included in the required position?
items.item_question_background: Availability statements are mandatory gate criteria in this framework.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the availability paragraph and verify placement after conclusions and before appendices/acknowledgments.

source_page_refs.quote: Inclusion of Code and/or data availability sections is mandatory for all papers...

items.item_question: If public release is restricted, are reasons stated and editor/reviewer access pathways documented?
items.item_question_background: Restricted public release does not remove peer-review access obligations.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract legal/licensing restrictions, stated reasons, and commitments for editor/reviewer access under anonymity constraints.

source_page_refs.quote: ...code and/or data must still be made available to the editor. Access must also be granted to the reviewers...

items.item_name: Development and technical paper evaluation depth
items.item_question: Do development/technical papers include substantial evaluation against benchmarks, observations, or model output?
items.item_question_background: Technical advances require evidence of practical modelling value.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract evaluation setup and outcomes showing the impact of the technical development.

source_page_refs.quote: Development and technical papers usually include a significant amount of evaluation...

items.item_question: For single-model development papers, are model name and version included in title?
items.item_question_background: Version-level identification remains mandatory beyond pure model-description papers.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Check title for explicit model/version naming in model-specific development case studies.

source_page_refs.quote: If the model development relates to a single model then the model name and the version number must be included in the title...

items.item_question: For assessment tools/method software, are model-description-like criteria and code availability satisfied?
items.item_question_background: Assessment pipelines can be model-like and need equivalent reproducibility controls.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract tool name/version, code access, and documentation elements required for replication.

source_page_refs.quote: Descriptions of software tools are subject to the same criteria as model descriptions...

items.item_question: For model-experiment papers, are protocol and boundary conditions versioned with data/code accessibility?
items.item_question_background: Experiment reproducibility requires versioned protocols and accessible inputs.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract experiment protocol version, boundary-condition versions, data availability, and links to supporting websites/papers.

source_page_refs.quote: ...experiment protocol should be given a version number; boundary conditions should be given a version number...

items.item_question: For pure evaluation papers, is there a published/in-review model description and proper citation linkage?
items.item_question_background: Evaluation-only papers are in-scope only under defined dependency conditions.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract citations and statements confirming prior or concurrent model/model-development description availability.

source_page_refs.quote: ...required that the model... has already been described in another paper (or that the description is also under review).

items.item_question: Does the manuscript clearly demonstrate a tangible advance and sufficient quality for full review?
items.item_question_background: Initial access review rejects out-of-scope or clearly deficient submissions early.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract editor-facing criteria evidence: tangible modelling advance, scope fit, and minimum quality threshold.

source_page_refs.quote: Manuscripts must contain a tangible advance in the field of geoscientific modelling...

items.item_question: Are similarity-report concerns addressed transparently, including grey literature and potential text overlap?
items.item_question_background: Similarity handling is a formal component of editorial decision quality.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract mention of prior internal reports, text overlap disclosures, and remediation/citation actions.

source_page_refs.quote: At the initial stage the editor must also study the similarity report...

items.item_question: Has code and supplementary material been accessed and, where feasible, compiled/run or inspected?
items.item_question_background: Operational checks strengthen reproducibility claims before acceptance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract evidence that code/download access, compilation/execution checks, or supplementary-file inspections were performed.

source_page_refs.quote: Editors should make sure that someone... has obtained the model or tool code. They may also attempt to compile the model...

EvidenceExamplesPositive: This model description paper includes model name/version in the title, a mandatory code-availability section, repository DOI for the release, benchmark and observational evaluation, and explicit discussion of scope and limitations.
Our GMD technical-development manuscript documents data assimilation code changes, provides reproducible test cases, and reports verification-versus-evaluation evidence for model updates.
The submitted MIP protocol paper provides experiment and boundary-condition versioning, data/code availability statements, and reproducible protocol details for independent models.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: A geophysics paper reports observational findings only, without model description, versioning, or code/data availability requirements.
A medical manuscript containing the acronym GMD discusses clinical outcomes and has no geoscientific modelling workflow.
A narrative climate-policy commentary references models broadly but provides no computational methods, reproducibility details, or model-version traceability.
Matching.Explain: Use as a geoscience discipline overlay when a manuscript presents geoscientific model development or model-related methods/protocol work and should satisfy explicit GMD-style traceability, reproducibility, and code/data-access publication requirements.
